# CoAIleague

CoAIleague is a B2B SaaS workforce management platform built exclusively for
the physical security guard industry. It replaces fragmented tools (GetSling,
Homebase, spreadsheets, phone calls, email chains) with a single intelligent
command center where AI handles 80%+ of routine decisions autonomously.

## What it does

- **Scheduling & coverage management** — Shift creation, publication, and
  conflict-free assignment for security officers across multiple sites.
  Automatic gap detection and coverage recommendations.
- **Time tracking & payroll** — GPS-verified clock-in/out, automatic
  overtime calculation across state jurisdictions, ACH direct deposit
  via Plaid, W-2 and 1099 contractor support.
- **Incident reporting & compliance** — Field officers submit incident
  reports from mobile. AI drafts legally articulate narratives, tracks
  post-order compliance per client site, and generates regulatory
  audit-ready documentation.
- **Officer license & training management** — State Private Security
  Bureau (PSB) license tracking, expiration alerts, training hour
  logging exportable in state-required formats, firearms endorsement
  verification.
- **Client communications & portals** — Dedicated portals for the
  security company's clients to view site status, officer rosters,
  incident summaries, and service reports. Automated weekly briefings.
- **Sales & proposal automation** — AI-generated, legally articulate
  security scope proposals, e-signature workflow, automated follow-up.
- **Trinity AI intelligence** — A single, self-aware operational AI
  agent (the central nervous system of the platform) with her own
  personality, reasoning, and memory. Trinity proactively scans the
  platform, flags risks, recommends coverage, drafts communications,
  and surfaces revenue opportunities. She operates under a Moral
  Compass rubric that prioritizes officer safety, truth to the client,
  and dignity for all parties. Trinity is one unified agent — not a
  collection of separate AIs — with interchangeable model backends
  that are implementation details, not product identity.

## Who it serves

CoAIleague serves three tiers of users in a multi-tenant architecture:

1. **Security companies (tenants)** — The business customers. They run
   their entire operation inside CoAIleague: scheduling, payroll,
   incidents, billing, client management, compliance.
2. **Security officers (end-users)** — Employees of tenant companies.
   They clock in/out, submit incident reports, acknowledge post orders,
   receive shift assignments, and communicate with supervisors through
   the platform.
3. **The tenant's clients** — Businesses that hire security companies.
   They access a dedicated client portal to monitor site coverage,
   review incident reports, and receive automated briefings.

## Public pages

- `/` — Landing page with product overview and primary CTA
- `/features` — Detailed feature breakdown by module
- `/pricing` — Subscription tier pricing (Free, Starter, Professional,
  Business, Enterprise)
- `/trinity-features` — Trinity AI capability overview
- `/compare` — Comparison against competitor platforms
- `/roi-calculator` — ROI calculator for security company operators
- `/contact` — Sales contact and demo request form
- `/support` — Support contact and help resources
- `/terms` — Terms of Service
- `/privacy` — Privacy Policy
- `/status` — Public service status page

## What CoAIleague does NOT do

- It is not a general workforce management platform. It is built
  specifically for physical security operations and will not serve
  unrelated industries well.
- It does not replace licensed security training providers. It tracks
  and documents training, but does not deliver the training itself.
- It does not act as a background check service. It integrates with
  Checkr and similar providers but does not issue background checks.
- It does not handle armed response dispatch for true emergency
  situations — those must route to local law enforcement.

## Key differentiators

- **Trinity autonomy** — 80%+ of routine operational decisions handled
  by AI without human intervention, under an auditable reasoning trace
  for every consequential decision.
- **Regulatory compliance infrastructure** — Audit-ready documentation
  designed to be recognized by state Private Security Bureaus and
  insurance auditors as a trusted reporting system.
- **Multi-state awareness** — Labor law, license requirements, and
  incident reporting formats adapt automatically per deployment state.
- **Officer wellness detection** — Trinity monitors burnout signals
  (consecutive days worked, response time degradation, call-off
  patterns) and flags them to supervisors as wellness check recommendations.
- **Christ-centered architecture** — Truth, dignity, and officer safety
  are enforced at the AI decision layer through an explicit ethical
  judge rubric, not as a mission statement.

## Contact

- Website: https://coaileague.com
- Support: support@coaileague.com
